allies.rationale_text: Metacognitive-questioning gives problem-framing-and-reframing the self-inspection needed to notice when the current lens is producing weak answers.
allies.source_quote: When approaching a problem, expert practitioners often start by reflecting on how the problem is framed, engaging in a form of metacognition.

allies.rationale_text: Systems-thinking helps reframing move from surface symptoms to better boundaries, interdependencies, and underlying structure.
allies.source_quote: Framing helps systems thinkers see the entire picture, searching for interdependencies and focusing on structure rather than fault.

allies.rationale_text: Anchoring is a useful ally only in reverse pressure here: it marks why reframing must generate alternatives before the first lens hardens into the answer.
allies.source_quote: They should be generated separately from solutions to prevent anchoring.

antagonists.rationale_text: Confirmation-bias resists reframing by defending the initial problem statement even after disconfirming evidence suggests a better cut.
antagonists.source_quote: Confirmation bias drives problem solvers to seek evidence that confirms their initial framing or hypothesis.

antagonists.rationale_text: Anchoring becomes an antagonist when the first framing or salient cue keeps the team from testing alternative boundaries and causal structures.
antagonists.source_quote: Anchoring and Substitution Bias: Focusing too much on a single, salient piece of information (the anchor).

antagonists.rationale_text: Representativeness-heuristic locks in the first pattern-matched frame by making it feel like the right one — the familiar structure activates fast and feels complete, which actively blocks the reframing process needed to find better causal boundaries.
antagonists.source_quote: problem-framing-and-reframing vs representativeness-heuristic: problem-framing-and-reframing conflicts with representativeness-heuristic (representativeness heuristic) when first familiar frame blocks alternative causal structures.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Problem-framing-and-reframing conflicts with confirmation-bias when reframing is constrained to preferred hypotheses.
structured_tensions.source_quote: problem-framing-and-reframing vs confirmation-bias: problem-framing-and-reframing conflicts with confirmation-bias (confirmation bias) when reframing is constrained to preferred hypotheses.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Problem-framing-and-reframing conflicts with representativeness-heuristic when the first familiar frame — activated by pattern resemblance — blocks alternative causal structures that reframing would otherwise reveal.
structured_tensions.source_quote: problem-framing-and-reframing vs representativeness-heuristic: problem-framing-and-reframing conflicts with representativeness-heuristic (representativeness heuristic) when first familiar frame blocks alternative causal structures.

curation_notes.summary: The raw source is strong on metacognitive frame-checking, systems boundary shifts, and explicit conflicts with confirmation-bias and representativeness-heuristic.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor reframing relations beyond the strongest current-vocabulary links around systems, self-inspection, and anti-lock-in pressure.
